content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COM MISSION 
428 J S1ree1 • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 3:!2-0886 
Steven T. Mattas 
Office of the City Attorney 
City Hall - 400 Grand Avenue 
South San Francisco, CA 94083 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-058 
Dear Mr. Mattas: 
April 11, 2012 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). The Act requires the Commission to provide 
written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question (Gov. Code, §83114, 
subd. (b); Cal. Code Regs., tit. 3, §18329, subd. (a)), the Commission may decline to provide 
advice where, as here, the advice would relate to past conduct. (Id., §18329, subd. (b)(8)(A).) 
After a careful review of lite facts provided in your letter dated April 2, 2012, we decline 
to provide written advice to you at this time. It is plain from the letter that the events relevant to 
your question transpired in 2006, and advice at this time would therefore relate to past conduct. 
JW:jgl 
If you have other questions concerning this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
ior Counsel. Legal Division

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts provided in your letter dated April 2, 2012, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. It is plain from the letter that the events relevant to your question transpired in 2006, and advice at this time would therefore relate to past conduct.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not provided in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the request relates to past conduct from 2006, which falls outside the scope of advice the Commission may provide under Government Code Section 83114 and California Code of Regulations Title 3, Section 18329(b)(8)(A).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. W-prefix on file number indicates withdrawal/declination. Request date April 2, 2012; response date April 11, 2012. Specific legal question not detailed in document.

QUESTION: The requestor sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not provided in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts provided in your letter dated April 2, 2012, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. It is plain from the letter that the events relevant to your question transpired in 2006, and advice at this time would therefore relate to past conduct.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to the City Attorney of South San Francisco regarding conflict-of-interest provisions because the underlying events occurred in 2006 and would constitute advice on past conduct.
